headline: Sosma Section 4 challenged in Federal Court test case
context: A landmark Federal Court case in 2025 challenges the constitutionality of SOSMA Section 4's 28-day detention without judicial review.

cards.text: Federal Court hears constitutional challenge to SOSMA Section 4 in 2025
cards.sub: Test case could determine whether 28-day detention violates Article 5

cards.h: SOSMA Section 4 allows 28-day detention with no requirement for judicial authorisation
cards.s: Only initial arrest requires reasonable suspicion under the Act
cards.d: SOSMA Section 4 allows 28-day detention with no requirement for judicial authorisation Only initial arrest requires reasonable suspicion under the Act

cards.h: Article 5 of Federal Constitution guarantees personal liberty to all persons
cards.s: Question is whether security exception in Article 149 overrides Article 5 protections
cards.d: Article 5 of Federal Constitution guarantees personal liberty to all persons Question is whether security exception in Article 149 overrides Article 5 protections

cards.h: Challenge brought by family of 26-year-old detainee held for 56 days in 2024
cards.s: Young man detained twice under SOSMA Section 4 without being charged
cards.d: Challenge brought by family of 26-year-old detainee held for 56 days in 2024 Young man detained twice under SOSMA Section 4 without being charged

cards.text: 56 days of detention without charge is the constitutional question made personal
9-member Federal Court bench convened, largest since Semenyih Jaya 2017 case
